title: Creamy Syrian Bryndza Cheese Kebabs
description: A rich and savory Syrian dish featuring bryndza cheese, perfect for a date night, with a jammy and chewy texture, and sugar-free friendly options.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the lamb meat, onion, garlic, salt, black pepper, and sumac, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
Crumble the bryndza cheese into the bowl and mix until the cheese is evenly distributed throughout the lamb mixture.
Thread the lamb and cheese mixture onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Brush the grill with olive oil to prevent sticking, then grill the kebabs for about 8-10 minutes per side, or until the lamb is cooked through.
Meanwhile, warm the pita bread by wrapping it in foil and heating it in the oven at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for a few minutes.
Serve the kebabs hot with the warm pita bread and your choice of toppings, such as chopped parsley or tomato.
Garnish with a sprinkle of sumac, if desired, and serve immediately.
